examples.title: KMP Algorithm - LPS Computation and Search
examples.description: Complete implementation of KMP algorithm in C showing preprocessing and searching phases
examples.explanation: This C implementation shows two main functions: computeLPS builds the LPS array in O(m) time, and KMPSearch uses it to find all occurrences in O(n) time. The algorithm never backtracks in the text.

examples.title: KMP Algorithm in Python
examples.description: Python implementation of KMP with detailed comments explaining each step
examples.explanation: The Python implementation demonstrates the two-phase approach clearly. compute_lps handles preprocessing in O(m) time, while kmp_search performs the actual searching in O(n) time.

examples.title: KMP Algorithm - Pseudocode
examples.description: Clean pseudocode representation of KMP algorithm for educational purposes
examples.code:
ALGORITHM ComputeLPS(P[0..m-1])
// P is the pattern, m is its length
// Returns LPS array
BEGIN
    SET LPS[0] ← 0
    SET len ← 0
    SET i ← 1
    
    WHILE i < m DO
        IF P[i] = P[len] THEN
            len ← len + 1
            LPS[i] ← len
            i ← i + 1
        ELSE
            IF len ≠ 0 THEN
                len ← LPS[len - 1]
            ELSE
                LPS[i] ← 0
                i ← i + 1
            END IF
        END IF
    END WHILE
    
    RETURN LPS
END ALGORITHM

ALGORITHM KMPSearch(T[0..n-1], P[0..m-1])
// T is text, P is pattern
BEGIN
    SET LPS ← ComputeLPS(P)
    SET i ← 0  // index for text
    SET j ← 0  // index for pattern
    
    WHILE i < n DO
        IF P[j] = T[i] THEN
            i ← i + 1
            j ← j + 1
        END IF
        
        IF j = m THEN
            PRINT "Pattern found at index", i - j
            j ← LPS[j - 1]
        ELSE IF i < n AND P[j] ≠ T[i] THEN
            IF j ≠ 0 THEN
                j ← LPS[j - 1]
            ELSE
                i ← i + 1
            END IF
        END IF
    END WHILE
END ALGORITHM
examples.explanation: This pseudocode clearly shows the LPS computation algorithm and the KMP search algorithm. The key insight is how LPS helps skip characters after a mismatch without backtracking in the text.

examples.title: KMP Algorithm in Java
examples.description: Complete Java implementation with object-oriented design
examples.explanation: This Java implementation provides a reusable class with search functionality. It demonstrates proper handling of edge cases and returns a list of all occurrences. The computeLPS method handles the preprocessing phase efficiently.
